name: FETAL NECK ANOMALIES
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF OBSTETRIC AND GYNECOLOGIC
definition: NUCHAL SONOLUCENCY / FULLNESS / EDEMA skin thickening of posterior neck measured between calvarium + dorsal skin margin

demographics.Prevalence: among the most common anomaly in 1st trimester + early 2nd trimester

dominant_finding: nuchal fullness ≥ 3 mm during 1st trimester is seen in trisomy 21 / 18 / 13 (30-50% PPV)
discriminating_features: larger lymphangiomas with radiating septations are usually found with trisomy 18
nuchal fullness ≥ 3 mm during 1st trimester is seen in trisomy 21 / 18 / 13 (30-50% PPV)
often reverting to normal by 16-18 weeks
septations within nuchal translucency carries a 20- to 200-fold risk for chromosomal
